passages.title: Read an article in a student magazine.

questions.question: According to the article, what philosophical perspective does kintsugi promote?

options.A: Minimalism, emphasizing simplicity and the elimination of excess
options.B: Ethics, focusing on honor and discipline in craftsmanship
options.C: Humility, valuing imperfection and the transient nature of life
options.D: Purity, highlighting spiritual cleanliness and ritual renewal

questions.question: What does the article suggest about the symbolic meaning of repairing pottery with precious metals?

options.A: Wealth and luxury are essential for authentic art.
options.B: Flaws can be turned into sources of value and beauty.
options.C: Artisans prioritize aesthetics over philosophical significance.
options.D: Objects lose their cultural value when restored with cheap materials.

questions.question: Why does the author of the article mention "a perfection-driven world"?

options.A: To argue that modern society has completely abandoned traditional art forms
options.B: To suggest that flawless craftsmanship is the goal of contemporary design
options.C: To contrast kintsugi's philosophical message with a common present-day attitude
options.D: To explain why kintsugi techniques are rarely used in professional pottery today

options.A: They must be disconnected from external devices.
options.B: They must be installed again after an update.
options.C: It is unlikely they will be lost during an update.
options.D: They can be accessed during an update.

questions.question: What can be concluded about the computer system during the process?

options.C: It will be available for remote work.

paragraphs: Approximation theory is a part of mathematics that helps us simplify complex problems. Instead of working with difficult functions directly, scientists use simpler ones that are "close enough" to get useful results. These simplified functions are often used to build algorithms—step-by-step procedures that solve problems efficiently. This is especially helpful in fields like computer science, where exact calculations can take too much time or power.

One common method is polynomial approximation, which uses basic math expressions (polynomials) to stand in for more complicated functions. A special type, called the Taylor series, breaks a function into an infinite sum based on its behavior at a single point. This helps us work with functions that are otherwise hard to handle.

Another key tool is the Fourier series, which decomposes repeating patterns—like sound waves—into a mix of sine and cosine waves. This is widely used in signal processing; engineers use Fourier series to filter out noise and enhance data quality.

While these methods are powerful, they are not perfect. Accuracy remains a challenge. The difference between the original and the simplified version—called the error—needs to be as small as possible. New techniques, like adaptive approximation, adjust the level of detail based on how accurate the result needs to be.

questions.question: According to the passage, simpler functions help scientists

options.C: tackle difficult functions more effectively over time
options.D: develop steps for solving problems relatively quickly

questions.question: What is indicated about polynomial approximation in the passage?

options.A: It varies in behavior from one point to another.
options.B: It is less accurate than the Fourier series.
options.C: It cannot handle infinite sums of terms as well as the Taylor series can.
options.D: It uses simple math expressions to make complex functions easier to work with.

questions.question: The word "decomposes" in the passage is closest in meaning to
questions.special_content: Another key tool is the Fourier series, which <mark>decomposes</mark> repeating patterns—like sound waves—into a mix of sine and cosine waves. This is widely used in signal processing; engineers use Fourier series to filter out noise and enhance data quality.

questions.question: What is one application of Fourier series mentioned in the passage?
Why does the author mention "the error"?

options.A: To help illustrate the issue of accuracy as it relates to approximation theory
options.B: To add another explanation of how polynomial approximation works
options.C: To demonstrate an additional benefit of the Fourier series
options.D: To suggest that new approximation techniques have so far been inadequate

questions.special_content: While these methods are powerful, they are not perfect. Accuracy remains a challenge. The difference between the original and the simplified version—called <mark>the error</mark>—needs to be as small as possible. New techniques, like adaptive approximation, adjust the level of detail based on how accurate the result needs to be.

paragraphs: Developmental Systems Theory (DST) is an approach within developmental biology that emphasizes the complex interactions between genetic, environmental, and epigenetic factors in shaping an organism's development. It challenges traditional views that either prioritize genes or environment in isolation. Instead, DST posits that development results from dynamic, reciprocal interactions among all these factors. Therefore, understanding an organism's development requires examining the full system rather than focusing on any single factor alone.

A key example highlighting DST involves the development of beak shapes in finches. Research indicates that while genetic factors play a significant role, environmental influences such as diet and climate are equally important. These interactions can lead to epigenetic changes—modifications in gene expression that do not alter the DNA sequence itself. For instance, finches consuming different diets may exhibit variations in beak shape due to changes in gene expression triggered by dietary components.

DST also introduces the concept of developmental plasticity, which refers to the ability of an organism to alter its development in response to varying environmental conditions. This adaptability is crucial for survival, especially in rapidly changing environments. Certain amphibians, for example, can modify their growth patterns based on resource availability.

questions.question: The word "reciprocal" in the passage is closest in meaning to
questions.special_content: Developmental Systems Theory (DST) is an approach within developmental biology that emphasizes the complex interactions between genetic, environmental, and epigenetic factors in shaping an organism's development. It challenges traditional views that either prioritize genes or environment in isolation. Instead, DST posits that development results from dynamic, <mark>reciprocal</mark> interactions among all these factors. Therefore, understanding an organism's development requires examining the full system rather than focusing on any single factor alone.

options.B: study the full system of development
options.C: maintain the superiority of genetic factors
options.D: deny the role of epigenetic factors

questions.question:
There are four locations [■] in the passage that indicate where the following sentence could be added.

Tadpoles of the Mexican spadefoot toad develop into omnivore morphs in nutrient-rich ponds with abundant plant material and into carnivore morphs in temporary ponds with plenty of animal prey.

Where would the sentence best fit? Select a location [■] to add the sentence to the passage.
questions.special_content: [■] DST also introduces the concept of developmental plasticity, which refers to the ability of an organism to alter its development in response to varying environmental conditions. [■] This adaptability is crucial for survival, especially in rapidly changing environments. [■] Certain amphibians, for example, can modify their growth patterns based on resource availability. [■]
questions.inserted_sentence: Tadpoles of the Mexican spadefoot toad develop into omnivore morphs in nutrient-rich ponds with abundant plant material and into carnivore morphs in temporary ponds with plenty of animal prey.
